slides.title: Welcome to Kindergarten!
slides.body: Kindergarten is a place to learn, play, and grow. Routines help you know what to do each day.

slides.title: Morning Routine
slides.body: In the morning, you hang up your backpack, say hello, and get ready to learn.

slides.title: Example: Lining Up
slides.body: When it’s time to go somewhere, you line up behind your classmates.

slides.title: Example: Asking for Help
slides.body: If you need help, you raise your hand and wait for your teacher.

slides.title: Example: Centers
slides.body: Centers are places where you practice reading, math, art, and more.

slides.prompt: What do you do when you need help?

feedback.correct: Yes! Raising your hand is the right way to ask for help.
feedback.incorrect: Try again. Think about what you do in class.

slides.prompt: Where do you put your backpack in the morning?

feedback.correct: Great job! Backpacks go on the hook.
feedback.incorrect: Not quite. Backpacks don’t go on the floor.

slides.prompt: What do you do when it’s time to go somewhere?

feedback.correct: Yes! You line up with your class.
feedback.incorrect: Try again. Think about moving together as a group.

slides.message: You’re learning how to follow routines. That helps everyone feel safe and ready to learn.

slides.title: You Know the Routines!
slides.message: Great job learning how to line up, ask for help, and start your day.
